linguisticChallenges.nastaliqScript: Urdu uses the Nastaliq calligraphic style of the Perso-Arabic script, which is more complex to render than Naskh (used for Arabic). Many digital fonts default to Naskh, producing readable but aesthetically wrong Urdu.
linguisticChallenges.hindiUrduContinuum: Urdu and Hindi are mutually intelligible in spoken form but use different scripts and draw vocabulary from different sources (Persian/Arabic for Urdu vs Sanskrit for Hindi). MT models sometimes produce Hindi-influenced vocabulary.
linguisticChallenges.rtlWithMixedScript: Like Arabic and Persian, Urdu faces bidi challenges with embedded LTR content.

dialects.classification: Standard Urdu based on the Khariboli dialect (same base as Hindi). Register differences come from vocabulary source (Persian/Arabic vs Sanskrit).

encyclopedic.history: Urdu developed as a literary register of Hindustani during the Mughal period, drawing heavily on Persian and Arabic vocabulary. It became the national language of Pakistan at independence in 1947.
